--- title: "Population Simulations" subtitle: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" module: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" ID: A13 date: last-modified institute: | ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100} format: esqlabs-revealjs --- ::: {.r-fit-text} ## **đźŽŻ Goal: Generate a virtual population and run a simulation across all individuals** ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ## Overview 1. Define population characteristics 2. Generate the population with `createPopulation()` 3. Export/import populations via CSV 4. Run a population simulation 5. Retrieve and plot results # Preparation ## Load Sample Simulation ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ospsuite) simFilePath <- system.file( "extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite" ) sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) ``` ::: # Define Population Characteristics ## `createPopulationCharacteristics()` Describes the population before sampling. Key arguments: - `species`, `population` - `numberOfIndividuals` - `proportionOfFemales` (percent, 0â€“100) - `weightMin` / `weightMax` (`weightUnit`, default `"kg"`) - `heightMin` / `heightMax` (`heightUnit`, default `"m"`) - `ageMin` / `ageMax` (`ageUnit`, default `"year(s)"`) - `moleculeOntogenies` ## Example ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} popCharacteristics <- createPopulationCharacteristics( species = Species$Human, population = HumanPopulation$Asian_Tanaka_1996, numberOfIndividuals = 50, proportionOfFemales = 50, weightMin = 30, weightMax = 98, weightUnit = "kg", ageMin = 0, ageMax = 80, ageUnit = "year(s)" ) popCharacteristics ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` â€˘ Species: Human â€˘ Population: Asian_Tanaka_1996 â€˘ Number of individuals: 50 â€˘ Proportion of females: 50 â€˘ Age: [0.00 year(s)..80.00 year(s)] â€˘ Gestational age: ]-Inf..+Inf[ â€˘ Weight: [30.00 kg..98.00 kg] â€˘ Height: ]-Inf..+Inf[ â€˘ BMI: ]-Inf..+Inf[ ``` ::: ::: The population argument selects a built-in physiological database from PK-Sim. Each entry in HumanPopulation corresponds to a published reference dataset (e.g., Asian_Tanaka_1996 is based on Tanaka et al. 1996). Run `names(HumanPopulation)` to see all available options. # Generate Population ## `createPopulation()` Samples all individuals at once. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} result <- createPopulation( populationCharacteristics = popCharacteristics ) myPopulation <- result$population myPopulation ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` â€˘ Number of Individuals: 50 ``` ::: ::: ## Inspect as Data Frame ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} popDf <- populationToDataFrame(myPopulation) dim(popDf) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 50 100 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} head(popDf[, 1:5]) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: Each row = one individual; columns are parameter paths. # CSV Round-Trip ## Export ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} populationToCSV( population = myPopulation, filePath = "myPopulation.csv" ) ``` ::: ## Import ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} loadedPop <- loadPopulation(csvPopulationFile = "myPopulation.csv") ``` ::: :::{.callout-tip} Use CSV to share populations across machines or reuse them without regenerating via PK-Sim. ::: # Run Population Simulation ## `runSimulations()` with `population` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} populationResults <- runSimulations( simulations = sim, population = myPopulation )[[1]] populationResults ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` â€˘ Number of individuals: 50 For paths: â€˘ Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) â€˘ Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound ``` ::: ::: ## Tune Parallelism ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} simRunOptions <- SimulationRunOptions$new() simRunOptions$numberOfCores <- 3 simRunOptions$showProgress <- TRUE populationResults <- runSimulations( simulations = sim, population = myPopulation, simulationRunOptions = simRunOptions )[[1]] ``` ::: Defaults: `numberOfCores = max(cores) - 1`, `showProgress = FALSE`. # Retrieve Results ## `getOutputValues()` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} resultsPath <- populationResults$allQuantityPaths[[1]] resultsData <- getOutputValues( populationResults, quantitiesOrPaths = resultsPath ) str(resultsData, max.level = 1) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` List of 2 $ data :'data.frame': 24550 obs. of 3 variables: $ metaData:List of 2 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} names(resultsData$data) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "IndividualId" [2] "Time" [3] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} head(resultsData$data) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: Columns: `IndividualId`, `Time`, plus one column per requested path. ## Subset Individuals ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} twoIndiv <- getOutputValues( populationResults, quantitiesOrPaths = resultsPath, individualIds = c(1, 2) ) unique(twoIndiv$data$IndividualId) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 1 2 ``` ::: ::: # Visualize ## Quick Plot ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} dc <- DataCombined$new() dc$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = populationResults, quantitiesOrPaths = resultsPath, names = "Population" ) ospsuite.plots::setDefaults() plotTimeProfile(dc) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A13-Population-Simulations_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){fig-align='center' width=2100} ::: ::: # Exercise ## Exercise Overview - Create a 20-individual European population, ages 25â€“65, weights 55â€“95 kg - Run the Aciclovir simulation for that population - Plot plasma profiles of individuals 1 and 2 ## Build Population and Run ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} popChar <- createPopulationCharacteristics( species = Species$Human, population = HumanPopulation$European_ICRP_2002, numberOfIndividuals = 20, proportionOfFemales = 50, weightMin = 55, weightMax = 95, ageMin = 25, ageMax = 65 ) pop <- createPopulation(populationCharacteristics = popChar)$population sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath) popResults <- runSimulations(simulations = sim, population = pop)[[1]] ``` ::: ## Plot Two Individuals ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} path <- popResults$allQuantityPaths[[1]] dc <- DataCombined$new() dc$addSimulationResults( simulationResults = popResults, quantitiesOrPaths = path, individualIds = c(1, 2), names = "European Population" ) plotTimeProfile(dc) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](A13-Population-Simulations_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){fig-align='center' width=2100} ::: ::: ## Resources - [`{ospsuite}` vignette on creating and running populations](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/create-run-population.html) - [PK-Sim populations documentation](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-creating-populations)

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
